headline: Cyril Imhof frames Anthropic's Claude Tag and its push into Microsoft Teams and Slack as the defining enterprise AI story this cycle.
body: Imhof returns repeatedly to Claude Tag, describing it as central to Anthropic's own internal operations and claiming 60% of Claude interactions run through it [12], while tracing how tagging is reshaping Slack and Teams workflows and organizational data management [10][13]. He probes the integration politics directly: Anthropic seeks a Teams tie-in even as Microsoft leverages its EU market position and Copilot to capture the broader agent market itself [4][7][8], and he argues firms can lock in licensing revenue by routing external agents through Model Context Protocols [5][6]. Alongside this, Cole Medin covers Anthropic's release of Claude Sonnet 5, calling it strong for its cost [1], and Imhof notes cheaper, faster Chinese models winning adoption on price and data sovereignty [9] plus a rapidly closing gap between open-source and frontier models [11]. He also questions whether wiring AI into every task beats dedicated software [0] and sketches AI that learns and acts asynchronously in ambient mode [3].
